factors.summary: Overall demand is expected to grow modestly in 2016, driven by increased unit shipments in the U.S. and higher sales in China.

evidence.text: We expect the U.S. residential water heater industry's unit shipments will be modestly higher in 2016 compared to 2015. We continue to see strength in U.S. commercial water heater volumes, and we expect the industry's 2016 unit shipments will be approximately five percent higher than last year. China sales in local currency grew 17 percent. We expect full year 2016 sales in China to grow at a rate of approximately 15 percent in local currency.

factors.summary: The company successfully raised prices in the U.S. and Canada by approximately 20% in 2015, which contributed to higher gross profit margins in 2016.

evidence.text: We increased prices approximately 20 percent in April 2015 for U.S. customers on NAECA III compliant products. First quarter gross profit margin of 41.2 percent in 2016 was higher than gross profit margin of 37.1 percent in the first quarter of 2015. The higher gross profit margin in 2016 was primarily due to price increases in the U.S. and Canada that were effective in April 2015 as well as lower material costs in the first quarter of 2016.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products, particularly in the North America segment, with increased sales of condensing boilers and specialty commercial water heating equipment.

evidence.text: Driven by what we believe is a continuing transition in the boiler industry from non-condensing boilers to condensing boilers as well as new product introductions, we expect sales of Lochinvar-branded products to grow approximately ten percent in 2016. North America segment also manufactures and globally markets specialty commercial water heating equipment, condensing and non-condensing boilers and water system tanks.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is expected to be between 9% to 9.5% in local currency and 7% to 7.5% in U.S. dollar terms in 2016, driven by growth in both North America and Rest of World segments.

evidence.text: We expect our businesses to grow between nine and 9.5 percent in local currency and between seven and 7.5 percent in U.S. dollar terms in 2016.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in the U.S. commercial water heater market, which is expected to increase by 5% in 2016. However, residential water heater volumes in the U.S. declined slightly in the first quarter of 2016.

evidence.text: The U.S. residential water heater industry's unit shipments will be modestly higher in 2016 compared to 2015. We continue to see strength in U.S. commercial water heater volumes, and we expect the industry's 2016 unit shipments will be approximately five percent higher than last year. North America Sales in the first quarter of 2016 declined approximately one percent driven primarily by lower volumes of U.S. residential water heaters.

factors.summary: The fastest-growing end markets are in China, where demand for water heaters, water treatment products, and air purifiers is increasing significantly. The U.S. commercial water heater market is also growing at a steady pace.

evidence.text: China sales in local currency grew 17 percent. We expect full year 2016 sales in China to grow at a rate of approximately 15 percent in local currency as we believe overall water heater market growth, geographic expansion, market share gains, growth in water treatment and air purification products and improved product mix will contribute to our growth. We continue to see strength in U.S. commercial water heater volumes, and we expect the industry's 2016 unit shipments will be approximately five percent higher than last year.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly steel prices, have increased significantly since January 1, 2016, which may negatively impact margins in the second half of 2016.

evidence.text: We expect full year depreciation and amortization will be approximately $70 million. Included in this guidance is a negative impact on margins beginning in the second half of 2016 due to the over $150 per ton increase in steel prices that has occurred since January 1, 2016.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving due to higher prices and lower material costs, but may face pressure from increased SG&A expenses and steel price increases.

evidence.text: First quarter gross profit margin of 41.2 percent in 2016 was higher than gross profit margin of 37.1 percent in the first quarter of 2015. The higher gross profit margin in 2016 was primarily due to price increases in the U.S. and Canada that were effective in April 2015 as well as lower material costs in the first quarter of 2016. North America operating earnings were $91.9 million in the first quarter of 2016 which were higher than operating earnings of $71.2 million in the same period of 2015. The operating margin in the first quarter of 2016 of 21.7 percent was higher than the operating margin of 16.6 percent in the same period last year.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: The company is implementing ERP systems, which may lead to incremental costs in 2016, but no specific measurable savings are mentioned.

evidence.text: We have successfully completed three enterprise resource planning (ERP) implementation milestones since 2014. We expect to have converted nearly all of our North American plant sites by the end of 2016. We project expenses related to our ERP implementation to be approximately $24 million in 2016 compared to $16 million of expenses in 2015 due to the larger number of scheduled implementation events in 2016.

factors.summary: Management has raised forward guidance for EPS to between $3.47 and $3.55 per share for 2016, reflecting increased confidence in performance.

evidence.text: With solid performance in the first quarter, we increased the midpoint of our EPS guidance for 2016. We believe we will achieve full-year earnings of between $3.47 and $3.55 per share, which excludes the potential impact from future acquisitions.

factors.summary: Management appears confident, as indicated by increased EPS guidance, share repurchase activity, and dividend payments.

evidence.text: Depending on factors such as stock price, working capital requirements and alternative investment opportunities, we expect to spend approximately $175 million on stock repurchase activity in 2016. On April 11, 2016, our Board of Directors declared a cash dividend of $0.24 per share on our Common Stock and Class A common stock.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include the over $150 per ton increase in steel prices, which may negatively impact margins starting in the second half of 2016.

evidence.text: Included in this guidance is a negative impact on margins beginning in the second half of 2016 due to the over $150 per ton increase in steel prices that has occurred since January 1, 2016.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to steel prices, with a significant increase expected to impact margins negatively in the second half of 2016.

evidence.text: Included in this guidance is a negative impact on margins beginning in the second half of 2016 due to the over $150 per ton increase in steel prices that has occurred since January 1, 2016.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: A key catalyst is the expected growth in China sales, driven by market expansion, product diversification, and improved product mix.

evidence.text: We expect full year 2016 sales in China to grow at a rate of approximately 15 percent in local currency as we believe overall water heater market growth, geographic expansion, market share gains, growth in water treatment and air purification products and improved product mix will contribute to our growth.

factors.summary: The company has foreign currency exposure, with the stronger U.S. dollar against Canadian and Chinese currencies impacting sales. However, the impact is partially offset by local currency growth in China.

evidence.text: Excluding the impact from the stronger U.S. dollar against the Canadian and Chinese currencies, sales increased approximately five percent in the first quarter of 2016 compared to the same period last year. China sales in local currency grew 17 percent.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to be between $110 to $120 million in 2016, including investments in ERP implementation and capacity expansion in China and the U.S.

evidence.text: We project 2016 capital expenditures will be between $110 to $120 million, including approximately $9 million to support our ERP software implementation and approximately $22 million related to capacity expansion to support growth in China and in the U.S.

factors.summary: Debt leverage increased slightly from 14.7% to 16.3% at the end of the first quarter of 2016, indicating a slight weakening of the balance sheet.

evidence.text: Our leverage, as measured by the ratio of total debt to total capitalization, was 16.3 percent at the end of the first quarter in 2016, compared with 14.7 percent at the end of last year.

factors.summary: The company is returning capital to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends, with plans to spend $175 million on buybacks in 2016 and a dividend increase.

evidence.text: Depending on factors such as stock price, working capital requirements and alternative investment opportunities, we expect to spend approximately $175 million on stock repurchase activity in 2016. On April 11, 2016, our Board of Directors declared a cash dividend of $0.24 per share on our Common Stock and Class A common stock.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation improved in the first quarter of 2016, with $26.5 million provided by operating activities, compared to $0.4 million used in the same period last year.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities in the first quarter of 2016 was $26.5 million compared with $0.4 million of cash used by operations during the same period last year.
